content: The operating region of the developed torque ( $T_{\mathrm{em}}$ ) and speed ( $\omega$ ) of an induction motor drive is given by the shaded region OQRE in the figure. The load torque $\left(T_L\right)$ characteristic is also shown. The motor drive moves from the initial operating point O to the final operating point S . Which one of the following trajectories will take the shortes time?
explanation:
The shortest time will be taken if motor follows the path that maximize the net accelerating torque (Tem- $T_L$ ) at all points.

Trajectories which takes shortest time.

content: A three phase, 50 Hz, 6 pole induction motor runs at 960 rpm. The stator copper loss, core loss, and the rotational loss of the motor can be neglected. The percentage efficiency of the motor is
explanation:
To determine the percentage efficiency of the induction motor, we need to calculate the motor's slip and then use it to find the rotor copper loss. Since stator copper loss, core loss, and rotational loss are neglected, the efficiency calculation becomes simpler.

First, we find the synchronous speed of the motor. The synchronous speed for a three-phase induction motor is given by:

$$ N_s = \frac{120 \times f}{P} $$

where:

  • $$N_s$$ is the synchronous speed in revolutions per minute (rpm)
  • $$f$$ is the frequency in hertz (Hz)
  • $$P$$ is the number of poles

Given:

  • $$f = 50 \, \text{Hz}$$
  • $$P = 6$$

Substituting the values, we get:

$$ N_s = \frac{120 \times 50}{6} = 1000 \, \text{rpm} $$

Next, we calculate the slip of the motor, which is defined as:

$$ s = \frac{N_s - N_r}{N_s} $$

where:

  • $$s$$ is the slip
  • $$N_r$$ is the rotor speed

Given:

  • $$N_r = 960 \, \text{rpm}$$

Therefore, the slip $$s$$ is:

$$ s = \frac{1000 - 960}{1000} = 0.04 $$

Now, to find the efficiency, we can use the following relationship:

$$ \eta = \left(1 - s\right) \times 100\% $$

Substituting the value of $$s$$, we get:

$$ \eta = \left(1 - 0.04\right) \times 100\% = 96\% $$

Therefore, the percentage efficiency of the motor is 96.

So, the correct option is:

Option C: 96

content: A 10-pole, 50 Hz, 240 V, single phase induction motor runs at 540 RPM while driving rated load. The frequency of induced rotor currents due to backward field is
explanation:
To determine the frequency of the induced rotor currents due to the backward field, we first need to understand a few key concepts about induction motors and their operation under load conditions.

The synchronous speed (ns) of an induction motor is given by the formula:

$$ n_s = \frac{120 \cdot f}{P} $$

where:

  • ns = Synchronous speed in revolutions per minute (RPM)
  • f = Supply frequency in Hertz (Hz)
  • P = Number of poles

For a 10-pole motor running at a supply frequency of 50 Hz, the synchronous speed can be calculated as follows:

$$ n_s = \frac{120 \cdot 50}{10} = 600 \, \text{RPM} $$

The motor operates at 540 RPM, which is slightly less than the synchronous speed due to slip. Slip (s) is given by the formula:

$$ s = \frac{n_s - n_r}{n_s} $$

where:

  • nr = Rotor speed in RPM

Plugging in the known values:

$$ s = \frac{600 - 540}{600} = \frac{60}{600} = 0.1 $$

The slip (s) is 0.1 or 10%. The frequency of the rotor currents in the forward field can be calculated as:

$$ f_r = s \cdot f $$

Substituting the values, we get:

$$ f_r = 0.1 \cdot 50 = 5 \, \text{Hz} $$

However, for the backward rotating field, the rotor currents operate at a slip of (2-s) with respect to the stator frequency. Thus, the frequency of induced rotor current due to the backward field can be calculated as follows:

$$ f_{b} = (2 - s) \cdot f $$

Substituting the known values, we get:

$$ f_{b} = (2 - 0.1) \cdot 50 = 1.9 \cdot 50 = 95 \, \text{Hz} $$

Therefore, the frequency of the induced rotor currents due to the backward field is 95 Hz. So, the correct answer is:

Option B: 95 Hz

content: The frequencies of the stator and rotor currents flowing in the three-phase 8-pole induction motor are 40 Hz and 1 Hz, respectively. The motor speed, in rpm, is ________. (round off to nearest integer).
explanation:
Stator frequency = 40 Hz, Rotor frequency = 1 Hz

Poles = 8, N = ?

sf = 1

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$s = {1 \over {40}} = 0.025$$

$${N_s} = {{120 \times 40} \over 8} = 600$$

N = Ns (1 $$-$$ s)

$$\therefore$$ N = 600 (1 $$-$$ 0.025)

= 585 rpm

content:
$$ \text { Consider the table given : } $$

Constructional feature Machine type Mitigation
P. Damper bars S. Induction motor X. Hunting
Q. Skewed rotor slots T. Transformer Y. Magnetic locking
R. Compensating winding U. Synchronous machine Z. Armature reaction
V. DC machine

The correct combination that relates the constructional feature, machine type and mitigation is
explanation:
(i) Damper bars are used in synchronous machines to eliminate hunting (P-U-X)

(ii) Skewed rotor slots are used in induction machine to reduce magnetic locking (cogging) (Q-S-Y)

(iii)Compensating winding is used in DC machine to neutralize armature reaction ( $\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{V}-\mathrm{Z}$ ).

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content:
The power input to a $500 \mathrm{~V}, 50 \mathrm{~Hz}, 6$ pole, 3 phase induction motor running at 975 rpm is 40 kW . The stator losses are 1 kW . If the total friction and windage losses are 2.025 kW , then the efficiency is

$\_\_\_\_$ $\%$.
explanation:
Given :

(i) Input power, $P_{\text {in }}=40 \mathrm{~kW}$

(ii) Voltage, $V=500 \mathrm{~V}$

(iii)Frequency $f=50 \mathrm{~Hz}$

(iv) Pole $P=6$

(v) Rotor speed, $N_r=975 \mathrm{rpm}$

(vi) The total stator losses are $1 \mathrm{~kW}=1000 \mathrm{~W}$

(vii) Total friction and windage loss $=2.025 \mathrm{~kW}=2025 \mathrm{~W}$

Air gap power, $P_g=P_{i n}-$ Stator losses

$$ P_g=40000-1000=39000 \mathrm{~W} $$

Rotor copper losses, $P_{c u}=s P_g$

$$ \begin{aligned}So,\,\,\, & P_m=P_g-s P_g \\ & P_m=(1-s) P_g \end{aligned} $$

Synchronous speed, $N_s=\frac{120 f}{P}$

$$ N_s=\frac{120 \times 50}{6}=1000 \mathrm{rpm} $$

So, slip   $$ s=\frac{N_s-N_r}{N_s}=\frac{1000-975}{1000}=0.025 $$

So,  $$ P_m=(1-0.025) \times 39000=38025 \mathrm{~W} $$

Shaft power,  $$ P_{s h}=P_m-\text { Frictional and windage losses } $$

$$ P_{s h}=38025-2025=36000 \mathrm{~W} $$

$$ \begin{aligned}Efficiency,\,\, & \eta=\frac{P_0}{P_{i n}}=\frac{P_{s h}}{P_{i n}} \\ & \% \eta=\frac{36000}{40000} \times 100=90 \% \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 90 .

A three phase slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side with stator winding short circuited. The frequency of the currents flowing in the short circuited stator is

A 3-phase, $400 \mathrm{~V}, 4$ pole, 50 Hz star connected induction motor has the following parameters referred to the stator :

$$ R_r^{\prime}=1 \Omega, X_S=X_r^{\prime}=2 \Omega $$

Stator resistance, magnetizing reactance and core loss of the motor are neglected. The motor is run with constant V/f control from a drive. For maximum starting torque, the voltage and frequency output, respectively, from the drive, is closest to,
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} Given :\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,& X_S=X_r^{\prime}=2 \Omega \\ & X_1=X_2^{\prime}=2 \Omega \end{aligned} $$

Condition for maximum starting torque :

$$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\ R_2^{\prime}=X_1+X_2^{\prime} $$

Let a frequency $F_m$ be the frequency. Corresponding to maximum $T_{s t}$.

$$ \begin{array}{r}\,\,\,\,\,\, 2 \pi f_m L_1+2 \pi f_m L_2^{\prime}=1 \\ 2 \pi f_m\left(L_1+L_2^{\prime}\right)=1 \end{array} $$

$$ \begin{array}{ll} & f_m=\frac{1}{2 \pi\left(L_1+L_2^{\prime}\right)} \\ \text { From given data }: & X_1=2 \pi L_1=2 \Omega \\ & X_2=2 \pi L_2^{\prime}=2 \Omega \\ & L_1=L_2^{\prime}=\frac{2}{2 \pi(50)}=6.369 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{H} \\ \therefore & f_m=\frac{1}{2 \pi\left(2 \times 6.369 \times 10^{-3}\right)}=12.5 \mathrm{~Hz} \end{array} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { V/F Control : V/F Ratio must remain same. }\\ &\therefore \quad \frac{V_2}{f_2}=\frac{V_1}{f_1} \Rightarrow V_2=\frac{400}{50} \times 12.5=100 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$

content:
A 3-phase star connected slip ring induction motor has the following parameters referred to the stator:

$R_s = 3\, \Omega,\; X_s = 2\, \Omega,\; X_r' = 2\, \Omega,\; R_r' = 2.5\, \Omega$

The per phase stator to rotor effective turns ratio is 3:1. The rotor winding is also star connected. The magnetizing reactance and core loss of the motor can be neglected. To have maximum torque at starting, the value of the extra resistance in ohms (referred to the rotor side) to be connected in series with each phase of the rotor winding is _______ (rounded off to 2 decimal places).

A three phase 415 V, 50 Hz, 6-pole, 960 RPM, 4 HP squirrel cage induction motor drives a constant torque load at rated speed operating from rated supply and delivering rated output. If the supply voltage and frequency are reduced by 20%, the resultant speed of the motor in RPM (neglecting the stator leakage impedance and rotational losses) is ___________ (Round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
Given : 4 HP Induction motor

$V_t=415 \mathrm{~V}, f_s=50 \mathrm{~Hz}, P=6, N_r=960 \mathrm{rpm}, T_L=$ Constant

$V$ and $f$ are reduced by $20 \%$

Hence, $\frac{V}{f}$ is constant

$$ T=\frac{3}{\omega_s} \frac{V^2}{\left(\frac{R_2^{\prime}}{s}\right)+X_2^2} \times \frac{R_2^{\prime}}{s} $$

As we know, motor operates in low slip region

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{R_2^{\prime}}{s} \gg X_2^{\prime} \text { so, } X_2^{\prime} \rightarrow \text { Neglect } \\\\ & T_d=\frac{3}{\omega_s} \frac{s V^2}{R_2^{\prime}} \end{aligned} $$

For constant load torque $T_d=$ Constant

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{s V^2}{\omega_s}=\text { Constant } \\ & \frac{s f^2}{f}=\text { Constant } \\\\ & s f=\text { Constant } \\\\ & N_s \propto f \\\\ & s N_s=\text { Constant } \\\\ & N_s-N_r=\text { Constant } \\\\ & N_{s_1}-N_{r_1}=N_{s_2}-N_{r_2} \\\\ & \frac{120 \times f_1}{P}-960=\frac{120 f_2}{P}-N_{r_2}\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{120 \times 50}{6}-960=\frac{120 f_2}{6}-N_{r_2} \\\\ & 1000-960=\frac{120 f_2}{6}-N_{r_2} \\\\ & 40=\frac{120 \times f_2}{6}-N_{r_2} \\\\ & \because f_2=0.8 f_1\end{aligned}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & f_2=0.8 \times 50=40 \mathrm{~Hz} \\\\ & \therefore 40=\frac{120 \times 40}{6}-N_{r_2} \\\\ & 40=800-N_{r_2} \\\\ & N_{r_2}=800-40=760 \mathrm{rpm} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the resultant speed of the motor is 760 rpm .

content: A 4-pole induction motor with inertia of 0.1 kg-m2 drives a constant load torque of 2 Nm. The speed of the motor is increased linearly from 1000 rpm to 1500 rpm in 4 seconds as shown in the figure below. Neglect losses in the motor. The energy, in joules, consumed by the motor during the speed change is _________. (round off to nearest integer).
explanation:
Equation of speed vs. time from 4 sec. to 8 sec.

N = 125t + 500 $$\Rightarrow$$ 4 sec $$\le$$ t $$\le$$ 8 sec

Now, $$J{{d\omega } \over {dt}} = {T_e} - {T_L}$$

or, $${T_e} = J{{d\omega } \over {dt}} + {T_L}$$

or, $$\omega {T_e} = J\omega {{d\omega } \over {dt}} + \omega {T_L}$$

or, $${P_e} = J\omega {{d\omega } \over {dt}} + \omega {T_L}$$

or, $${{dE} \over {dt}} = J\omega {{d\omega } \over {dt}} + \omega {T_L}$$

or, $$dE = J\omega d\omega + {T_L}\omega dt$$

or, $$E = J\int {\omega d\omega + {T_L}\int {\omega dt} } $$

or, $$E = J{\left( {{{2\pi } \over {60}}} \right)^2}\int\limits_{1000}^{1500} {NdN + {T_L}{{2\pi } \over {60}}\int\limits_4^8 {Ndt} } $$

$$ = J{\left( {{{2\pi } \over {60}}} \right)^2}\left( {{{{N^2}} \over 2}} \right)_{1000}^{1500} + 2.{{2\pi } \over {60}}\int\limits_4^8 {(125t + 500)dt} $$

$$ = 0.1 \times {\left( {{{2\pi } \over {60}}} \right)^2}\left( {{{{{1500}^2} - {{1000}^2}} \over 2}} \right) + 2.{{2\pi } \over {60}}\left[ {{{125{t^2}} \over 2} + 500t} \right]_4^8$$

$$ = 685.389 + 1047.197$$

$$ = 1732.5865$$ J

content: A 3-phase, 415 V, 50 Hz induction motor draws 5 times the rated current at rated voltage at starting. It is required to bring down the starting current from the supply to 2 times of the rated current using a 3-phase autotransformer. If the magnetizing impedance of the induction motor and no load current of the autotransformer is neglected, then the transformation ratio of the autotransformer is given by ________. (round off to two decimal places).
explanation:
Given : Isc = 5 Irated $$\Rightarrow$$ Isc = 5If

It is required to make starting current to "2If" using auto transformer.

Line current with auto transformer,

$${I_L} = {x^2}{I_{sc}}$$

Given : $${I_L} = 2{I_f} \Rightarrow 2{I_f} = {x^2}{I_{sc}}$$

$${{2{I_f}} \over {{I_{sc}}}} = {x^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} = 2 \times \left( {{1 \over 5}} \right) \Rightarrow x = \sqrt {{2 \over 5}} = 0.63$$

content: An 8 pole, $50 \mathrm{~Hz}, 3$ phase, slip-ring induction motor has an effective rotor resistance of $0.08 \Omega$ per phase. Its speed at maximum torque is 650 rpm . The additional resistance per phase that must be inserted in the rotor to achieve maximum torque at start is $\_\_\_\_$ $\Omega$. (Round off to 2 decimal places). Negleet magnetizing eurrent and stater leakage impedanee. Consider equivalent eireuit parameters referred to stator.
explanation:
$$ \text { From torque-slip characteristic of } 3-\phi \text { induction motor, } $$

Given : Three phase induction motor

(i) Poles, $P=8$

(ii) Frequency, $f=50 \mathrm{~Hz}$

(iii)Rotor resistance, $R_2=0.08 \Omega /$ phase

(iv)Speed corresponding to maximum torque $=650 \mathrm{rpm}$

$$ N_x=\frac{120 f}{p}=\frac{120 \times 50}{8}=750 \mathrm{rpm} $$

Speed corresponding to maximum torque,

$$ \begin{aligned} & s_m=\frac{N_s-N_r}{N_s} \\ & s_m=\frac{750-650}{750}=0.133 \end{aligned} $$

Rotor resistance/phase, $R_2=0.08 \Omega$

$$ s_m=\frac{R_2}{X_2} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$$

The additional resistance per phase that must be inserted in the rotor circuit to achieve maximum torque at start is $R_{\text {ext }}$.

To obtain the maximum torque at starting, maximum slip, $S_m=1$.

$$ s_m=\frac{R_2+R_{e x t}}{X_2} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)$$

From equation (i) and (ii),

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{R_2}{S_m}=\frac{R_2+R_{e x t}}{S_{m \text { (atstarting) }}} \\ & \frac{0.08}{0.133}=\frac{0.08+R_{e x t}}{1} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &R_{e x t}=0.52 \Omega\\ &\text { Hence, the correct answer is } 0.52 \text {. } \end{aligned} $$

both the speed and the stator current decrease
the speed and the stator current remain practically constant
there is negligible change in the speed but the stator current decreases

content:
Assertion [a]: Under V/f control of induction motor, the maximum value of the developed torque remains constant over a wide range of speed in the subsynchronous region.

Reason [r]: The magnetic flux is maintained almost constant at the rated value by keeping the ratio V/f constant over the considered speed range.

options.content: Both [a] and [r] are true and [r] is the correct reason for [a]
Both [a] and [r] are true but [r] is not the correct reason for [a]
